text: And the priest perceive this, he shall condemn him undoubtedly of leprosy which is risen in the bald part.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now whosoever shall be defiled with the leprosy, and is separated by the judgment of the priest:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Shall have his clothes hanging loose, his head bare, his mouth covered with a cloth: and he shall cry out that he is defiled and unclean.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All the time that he is a leper and unclean he shall dwell alone without the camp.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A woollen or linen garment that shall have the leprosy

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the warp, and the woof: or skin, or whatsoever is made of a skin:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If it be infected with a white or red spot, it shall be accounted the leprosy, and shall be shewn to the priest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall look upon it and shall shut it up seven days.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And on the seventh day when he looketh on it again, if he find that it is grown, it is a fixed leprosy. He shall judge the garment unclean, and every thing wherein it shall be found.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And therefore it shall be burnt with fire.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if he see that it is not grown,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall give orders, and they shall wash that part wherein the leprosy is: and he shall shut it up other seven days.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he shall see that the former colour is not returned, nor yet the leprosy spread, he shall judge it unclean: and shall burn it with fire, for the leprosy has taken hold of the outside of the garment, or through the whole.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if the place of the leprosy be somewhat dark, after the garment is washed, he shall tear it off, and divide it from that which is sound.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if after this there appear in those places that before were without spot, a flying and wandering leprosy: it must be burnt with fire.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If it cease, he shall wash with water the parts that are pure, the second time: and they shall be clean.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the law touching the leprosy of any woollen or linen garment, either in the warp or woof, or any thing of skins: how it ought to be cleaned, or pronounced unclean.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the rite of a leper, when he is to be cleansed. He shall be brought to the priest:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who going out of the camp, when he shall find that the leprosy is cleansed,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Shall command him that is to be purified, to offer for himself two living sparrows, which it is lawful to eat, and cedar wood, and scarlet, and hyssop.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall command one of the sparrows to be immolated in an earthen vessel over living waters.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the other that is alive, he shall dip, with the cedar wood, and the scarlet and the hyssop, in the blood of the sparrow that is immolated:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Wherewith he shall sprinkle him that is to be cleansed seven times, that he may be rightly purified. And he shall let go the living sparrow, that it may fly into the field.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the man hath washed his clothes, he shall shave all the hair of his body, and shall be washed with water: and being purified he shall enter into the camp, yet so that he tarry without his own tent seven days.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And on the seventh day he shall shave the hair of his head, and his beard and his eyebrows, and the hair of all his body. And having washed again his clothes, and his body,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: On the eighth day, he shall take two lambs without blemish, and an ewe of a year old without blemish, and three tenths of flour tempered with oil for a sacrifice, and a sextary of oil apart.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the priest that purifieth the man, hath presented him, and all these things before the Lord, at the door of the tabernacle of the testimony:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall take a lamb, and offer it for a trespass offering with the sextary of oil. And having offered all before the Lord,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall immolate the lamb, where the victim for sin is wont to be immolated, and the holocaust, that is, in the holy place. For as that which is for sin, so also the victim for a trespass offering pertaineth to the priest: it is holy of holies.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the priest taking of the blood of the victim that was immolated for trespass, shall put it upon the tip of the right ear of him that is cleansed, and upon the thumb of his right hand and the great toe of his right foot.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall pour of the sextary of oil into his own left hand,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And shall dip his right finger in it, and sprinkle it before the Lord seven times.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the rest of the oil in his left hand, he shall pour upon the tip of the right ear of him that is cleansed, and upon the thumb of his right hand and the great toe of his right foot, and upon the blood that was shed for trespass:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And upon his head.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall pray for him before the Lord, and shall offer the sacrifice for sin. Then shall he immolate the holocaust.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And put it on the altar with the libations thereof: and the man shall be rightly cleansed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if he be poor, and his hand cannot find the things aforesaid: he shall take a lamb for an offering for trespass, that the priest may pray for him, and a tenth part of flour tempered with oil for a sacrifice, and a sextary of oil:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And two turtles or two young pigeons, of which one may be for sin, and the other for a holocaust.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall offer them on the eighth day of his purification to the priest, at the door of the tabernacle of the testimony before the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the priest receiving the lamb for trespass, and the sextary of oil, shall elevate them together.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the lamb being immolated, he shall put of the blood thereof upon the tip of the right ear of him that is cleansed, and upon the thumb of his right hand, and the great toe of his right foot.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he shall pour part of the oil into his own left hand,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And dipping the finger of his right hand in it, he shall sprinkle it seven times before the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall touch the tip of the right ear of him that is cleansed, and the thumb of his right hand and the great toe of his right foot, in the place of the blood that was shed for trespass.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the other part of the oil that is in his left hand, he shall pour upon the head of the purified person, that he may appease the Lord for him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall offer a turtle, or young pigeon:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: One for trespass, and the other for a holocaust, with their libations.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the sacrifice of a leper, that is not able to have all things that appertain to his cleansing.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When you shall come into the land of Chanaan, which I will give you for a possession, if there be the plague of leprosy in a house:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He whose house it is, shall go and tell the priest, saying: It seemeth to me, that there is the plague of leprosy in my house,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall command, that they carry forth all things out of the house, before he go into it, and see whether it have the leprosy, let all things become unclean that are in the house. And afterwards he shall go in to view the leprosy of the house.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if he see in the walls thereof as it were little dints, disfigured with paleness or redness, and lower than all he rest:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall go out of the door of the house, and forthwith shut it up seven days,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And returning on the seventh day, he shall look upon it. If he find that the leprosy is spread,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall command, that the stones wherein the leprosy is, be taken out, and cast without the city into an unclean place:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And that the house be scraped on the inside round about, and the dust of the scrapings be scattered without the city into an unclean place:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And that other stones be laid in the place of them that were taken away, and the house be plastered with other mortar.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if after the stones be taken out, and the dust scraped off, and it be plastered with other earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The priest going in perceive that the leprosy is returned, and the walls full of spots, it is a lasting leprosy, and the house is unclean.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall destroy it forthwith, and shall cast the stones and timber thereof, and all the dust without the town into an unclean place.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He that entereth into the house when it is shut, shall be unclean until evening,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he that sleepeth in it, and eateth any thing, shall wash his clothes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if the priest going in perceive that the leprosy is not spread in the house, after it was plastered again, he shall purify it, it being cured.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And for the purification thereof he shall take two sparrows, and cedar wood, and scarlet, and hyssop.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And having immolated one sparrow in an earthen vessel, over living waters,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall take the cedar wood, and the hyssop, and the scarlet, and the living sparrow, and shall dip all in the blood of the sparrow that is immolated, and in the living water: and he shall sprinkle the house seven times.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And shall purify it as well with the blood of the sparrow, as with the living water, and with the living sparrow, and with the cedar wood, and the hyssop, and the scarlet.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he hath let go the sparrow to fly freely away into the field, he shall pray for the house: and it shall be rightly cleansed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the law of every kind of leprosy and stroke.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the leprosy of garments and houses,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of a scar and of blisters breaking out of a shining spot, and when the colours are diversely changed:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That it may be known when a thing is clean or unclean.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Speak to the children of Israel, and say to them: The man that hath an issue of seed, shall be unclean.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And then shall he be judged subject to this evil, when a filthy humour, at every moment, cleaveth to his flesh, and gathereth there.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Every bed on which he sleepeth, shall be unclean, and every place on which he sitteth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If any man touch his bed, he shall wash his clothes and being washed with water, he shall be unclean until the evening.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If a man sit where that man hath sitten, he also shall wash his clothes: and being washed with water, shall be unclean until the evening.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He that toucheth his flesh, shall wash his clothes: and being himself washed with water shall be unclean until the evening.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If such a man cast his spittle upon him that is clean, he shall wash his clothes: and being washed with water, he shall be unclean until the evening.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The saddle on which he hath sitten shall be unclean.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And whatsoever has been under him that hath the issue of seed, shall be unclean until the evening. He that carrieth any of these things, shall wash his clothes: and being washed with water, he shall be unclean until the evening.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Every person whom such a one shall touch, not having washed his hands before, shall wash his clothes: and being washed with water, shall be unclean until the evening.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If he touch a vessel of earth, it shall be broken: but if a vessel of wood, it shall be washed with water.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If he who suffereth this disease be healed, he shall number seven days after his cleansing: and having washed his clothes, and all his body in living water, he shall be clean.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And on the eighth day he shall take two turtles, or two young pigeons, and he shall come before the Lord, to the door of the tabernacle of the testimony, and shall give them to the priest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who shall offer one for sin, and the other for a holocaust: and he shall pray for him before the Lord, that he may be cleansed of the issue of his seed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The man from whom the seed of copulation goeth out, shall wash all his body with water: and he shall be unclean until the evening.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The garment or skin that he weareth, he shall wash with water: and it shall be unclean until the evening.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The woman, with whom he copulateth, shall be washed with water: and shall be unclean until the evening.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The woman, who at the return of the month, hath her issue of blood, shall be separated seven days.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Every one that toucheth her, shall be unclean until the evening.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And every thing that she sleepeth on, or that she sitteth on in the days of her separation, shall be defiled.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He that toucheth her bed shall wash his clothes: and being himself washed with water, shall be unclean until the evening.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whosoever shall touch any vessel on which she sitteth, shall wash his clothes: and himself being washed with water, shall be defiled until the evening.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If a man copulateth with her in the time of her flowers, he shall be unclean seven days: and every bed on which he shall sleep, shall be defiled.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The woman that hath still issue of blood many days out of her ordinary time, or that ceaseth not to flow after the monthly courses, as long as she is subject to this disease, shall be unclean, in the same manner as if she were in her flowers.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Every bed on which she sleepeth, and every vessel on which she sitteth, shall be defiled.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whosoever toucheth them shall wash his clothes: and himself being washed with water, shall be unclean until the evening.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If the blood stop and cease to run, she shall count seven days of her purification:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And on the eighth day she shall offer for herself to the priest, two turtles, or two young pigeons, at the door of the tabernacle of the testimony:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall offer one for sin, and the other for a holocaust, and he shall pray for her before the Lord, and for the issue of her uncleanness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You shall teach therefore the children of Israel to take heed of uncleanness, that they may not die in their filth, when they shall have defiled my tabernacle that is among them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the law of him that hath the issue of seed, and that is defiled by copulation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And of the woman that is separated in her monthly times, or that hath a continual issue of blood, and of the man that sleepeth with her.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, after the death of the two sons of Aaron when they were slain upon their offering strange fire:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he commanded him, saying: Speak to Aaron thy brother, that he enter not at all into the sanctuary, which is within the veil before the propitiatory, with which the ark is covered, lest he die, (for I will appear in a cloud over the oracle),

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Unless he first do these things. He shall offer a calf for sin, and a ram for a holocaust.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall be vested with a linen tunick: he shall cover his nakedness with linen breeches: he shall be girded with a linen girdle, and he shall put a linen mitre upon his head. For these are holy vestments: all which he shall put on, after he is washed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall receive from the whole multitude of the children of Israel two buck goats for sin, and one ram for a holocaust.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he hath offered the cattle and prayed for himself and for his own house:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall make the two buck goats to stand before the Lord in the door of the tabernacle of the testimony.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And casting lots upon them both, one to be offered to the Lord, and the other to be the emissary goat:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That whose lot fell to be offered to the Lord, he shall offer for sin.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But that whose lot was to be the emissary goat, he shall present before the Lord, that he may pour prayers upon him, and let him go into the wilderness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: After these things are duly celebrated, he shall offer the calf: and praying for himself and for his own house, he shall immolate it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And taking the censer, which he hath filled with the burning coals of the altar, and taking up with his hands the compounded perfume for incense, he shall go in within the veil into the holy place:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That when the perfumes are put upon the fire, the cloud and vapour thereof may cover the oracle, which is over the testimony, and he may not die.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall take also of the blood of the calf, and sprinkle with his finger seven times towards the propitiatory to the east.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he hath killed the buck goat for the sin of the people, he shall carry in the blood thereof within the veil, as he was commanded to do with the blood of the calf, that he may sprinkle it over against the oracle:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And may expiate the sanctuary from the uncleanness of the children of Israel, and from their transgressions, and all their sins. According to this rite shall he do to the tabernacle of the testimony, which is fixed among them in the midst of the filth of their habitation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let no man be in the tabernacle when the high priest goeth into the sanctuary, to pray for himself and his house, and for the whole congregation of Israel, until he come out.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he is come out to the altar that is before the Lord, let him pray for himself: and taking the blood of the calf, and of the buck goat, let him pour it upon the horns thereof round about.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And sprinkling with his finger seven times, let him expiate, and sanctify it from the uncleanness of the children of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: After he hath cleaned the sanctuary, and the tabernacle, and the altar, then let him offer the living goat.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And putting both hands upon his head, let him confess all the iniquities of the children of Israel, and all their offences and sins. And praying that they may light on its head, he shall turn him out by a man ready for it, into the desert.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the goat hath carried all their iniquities into an uninhabited land, and shall be let go into the desert:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Aaron shall return into the tabernacle of the testimony, and putting off the vestments, which he had on him before when he entered into the sanctuary, and leaving them there,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall wash his flesh in the holy place, and shall put on his own garments. And after that he is come out and hath offered his own holocaust, and that of the people, he shall pray both for himself, and for the people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the fat that is offered for sins, he shall burn on the altar.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he that hath let go the emissary goat, shall wash his clothes, and his body with water, and so shall enter into the camp.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the calf and the buck goat, that were sacrificed for sin, and whose blood was carried into the sanctuary, to accomplish the atonement, they shall carry forth without the camp, and shall burn with fire: their skins and their flesh, and their dung.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And whosoever burneth them shall wash his clothes, and flesh with water: and so shall enter into the camp.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And this shall be to you an everlasting ordinance. The seventh month, the tenth day of the month, you shall afflict your souls, and shall do no work, whether it be one of your own country, or a stranger that sojourneth among you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Upon this day shall be the expiation for you, and the cleansing from all your sins. You shall be cleansed before the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For it is a sabbath of rest: and you shall afflict your souls by a perpetual religion.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the priest that is anointed, and whose hands are consecrated to do the office of the priesthood in his father’s stead, shall make atonement. And he shall be vested with the linen robe and the holy vestments.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall expiate the sanctuary and the tabernacle of the testimony and the altar: the priest also and all the people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And this shall be an ordinance for ever, that you pray for the children of Israel, and for all their sins once a year. He did therefore as the Lord had commanded Moses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Speak to Aaron and his sons, and to all the children of Israel, saying to them: This is the word, which the Lord hath commanded, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Any man whosoever of the house of Israel, if he kill an ox, or a sheep, or a goat in the camp, or without the camp,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And offer it not at the door of the tabernacle an oblation to the Lord, shall be guilty of blood. As if he had shed blood, so shall he perish from the midst of his people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore the children of Israel shall bring to the priest their victims, which they kill in the field, that they may be sanctified to the Lord before the door of the tabernacle of the testimony: and they may sacrifice them for peace offerings to the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the priest shall pour the blood upon the altar of the Lord, at the door of the tabernacle of the testimony: and shall burn the fat for a sweet odour to the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall no more sacrifice their victims to devils, with whom they have committed fornication. It shall be an ordinance for ever to them and to their posterity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt say to them: The man of the house of Israel, and of the strangers who sojourn among you, that offereth a holocaust or a victim,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And bringeth it not to the door of the tabernacle of the testimony, that it may be offered to the Lord, shall perish from among his people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If any man whosoever of the house of Israel, and of the strangers that sojourn among them, eat blood, I will set my face against his soul, and will cut him off from among his people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because the life of the flesh is in the blood: and I have given it to you, that you may make atonement with it upon the altar for your souls, and the blood may be for an expiation of the soul.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore I have said to the children of Israel: No soul of you, nor of the strangers that sojourn among you, shall eat blood.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Any man whosoever of the children of Israel, and of the strangers that sojourn among you, if by hunting or fowling, he take a wild beast or a bird, which is lawful to eat, let him pour out its blood, and cover it with earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the life of all flesh is in the blood. Therefore I said to the children of Israel: you shall not eat the blood of any flesh at all, because the life of the flesh is in the blood, and whosoever eateth it, shall be cut off.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The soul that eateth that which died of itself, or has been caught by a beast, whether he be one of your own country or a stranger, shall wash his clothes and himself with water, and shall be defiled until the evening: and in this manner he shall be made clean.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if he do not wash his clothes, and his body, he shall bear his iniquity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Speak to the children of Israel, and thou shalt say to them: I am the Lord your God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You shall not do according to the custom of the land of Egypt, in which you dwelt: neither shall you act according to the manner of the country of Chanaan, into which I will bring you. Nor shall you walk in their ordinances.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You shall do my judgments, and shall observe my precepts, and shall walk in them. I am the Lord your God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Keep my laws and my judgments: which if a man do, he shall live in them, I am the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: No man shall approach to her that is near of kin to him, to uncover her nakedness. I am the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of thy father, or the nakedness of thy mother: she is thy mother, thou shalt not uncover her nakedness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of thy father’s wife: for it is the nakedness of thy father.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of thy sister by father or by mother: whether born at home or abroad.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of thy son’s daughter, or thy daughter’s daughter: because it is thy own nakedness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of thy father’s wife’s daughter, whom she bore to thy father: and who is thy sister.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of thy father’s sister: because she is the flesh of thy father.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of thy mother’s sister: because she is thy mother’s flesh.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of thy father’s brother: neither shalt thou approach to his wife, who is joined to thee by affinity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of thy daughter in law: because she is thy son’s wife, neither shalt thou discover her shame.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of thy brother’s wife: because it is the nakedness of thy brother.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of thy wife and her daughter. Thou shalt not take her son’s daughter or her daughter’s daughter, to discover her shame: because they are her flesh, and such copulation is incest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not take thy wife’s sister for a harlot, to rival her: neither shalt thou discover her nakedness, while she is yet living.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not approach to a woman having her flowers: neither shalt thou uncover her nakedness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not lie with thy neighbour’s wife: nor be defiled with mingling of seed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not give any of thy seed to be consecrated to the idol Moloch, nor defile the name of thy God. I am the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not lie with mankind as with womankind: because it is an abomination.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not copulate with any beast: neither shalt thou be defiled with it. A woman shall not lie down to a beast, nor copulate with it: because it is a heinous crime.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Defile not yourselves with any of these things with which all the nations have been defiled, which I will cast out before you,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And with which the land is defiled: the abominations of which I will visit, that it may vomit out its inhabitants.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Keep ye my ordinances and my judgments: and do not any of these abominations. Neither any of your own nation, nor any stranger that sojourneth among you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For all these detestable things the inhabitants of the land have done, that were before you, and have defiled it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Beware then, lest in like manner, it vomit you also out, if you do the like things: as it vomited out the nation that was before you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Every soul that shall commit any of these abominations, shall perish from the midst of his people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Keep my commandments. Do not the things which they have done, that have been before you: and be not defiled therein. I am the Lord your God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Speak to all the congregation of the children of Israel. And thou shalt say to them: Be ye holy, because I the Lord your God am holy.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let every one fear his father, and his mother. Keep my sabbaths. I am the Lord your God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Turn ye not to idols: nor make to yourselves molten gods. I am the Lord your God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If ye offer in sacrifice a peace offering to the Lord, that he may be favourable:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You shall eat it on the same day it was offered, and the next day. And whatsoever shall be left until the third day, you shall burn with fire.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If after two days any man eat thereof, he shall be profane and guilty of impiety:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And shall bear his iniquity, because he hath defiled the holy thing of the Lord. And that soul shall perish from among his people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When thou reapest the corn of thy land, thou shalt not cut down all that is on the face of the earth to the very ground: nor shalt thou gather the ears that remain.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Neither shalt thou gather the bunches and grapes that fall down in thy vineyard: but shalt leave them to the poor and the strangers to take. I am the Lord your God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You shall not steal. You shall not lie: neither shall any man deceive his neighbour.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not swear falsely by my name, nor profane the name of thy God. I am the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not calumniate thy neighbour, nor oppress him by violence. The wages of him that hath been hired by thee shall not abide with thee until the morning.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not speak evil of the deaf, nor put a stumbling block before the blind: but thou shalt fear the Lord thy God, because I am the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not do that which is unjust, nor judge unjustly. Respect not the person of the poor: nor honour the countenance of the mighty. But judge thy neighbour according to justice.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not be a detractor nor a whisperer among the people. Thou shalt not stand against the blood of thy neighbour. I am the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not hate thy brother in thy heart: But reprove him openly, lest thou incur sin through him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Seek not revenge, nor be mindful of the injury of thy citizens. Thou shalt love thy friend as thyself. I am the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Keep ye my laws. Thou shalt not make thy cattle to gender with beasts of any other kind. Thou shalt not sow thy field with different seeds. Thou shalt not wear a garment that is woven of two sorts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If a man carnally lie with a woman that is a bondservant and marriageable, and yet not redeemed with a price, nor made free: they both shall be scourged: and they shall not be put to death, because she was not a free woman.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And for his trespass he shall offer a ram to the Lord, at the door of the tabernacle of the testimony.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the priest shall pray for him: and for his sin before the Lord: and he shall have mercy on him, and the sin shall be forgiven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When you shall be come into the land, and shall have planted in it fruit trees, you shall take away the firstfruits of them. The fruit that comes forth shall be unclean to you: neither shall you eat of them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But in the fourth year, all their fruit shall be sanctified, to the praise of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in the fifth year you shall eat the fruits thereof, gathering the increase thereof. I am the Lord your God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You shall not eat with blood. You shall not divine nor observe dreams.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Nor shall you cut your hair roundwise: nor shave your beard.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You shall not make any cuttings in your flesh, for the dead: neither shall you make in yourselves any figures or marks. I am the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Make not thy daughter a common strumpet, lest the land be defiled, and filled with wickedness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Keep ye my sabbaths, and reverence my sanctuary. I am the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Go not aside after wizards: neither ask any thing of soothsayers, to be defiled by them. I am the Lord your God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Rise up before the hoary head, and honour the person of the aged man: and fear the Lord thy God. I am the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If a stranger dwell in your land, and abide among you, do not upbraid him:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But let him be among you as one of the same country. And you shall love him as yourselves: for you were strangers in the land of Egypt. I am the Lord your God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Do not any unjust thing in judgment, in rule, in weight, or in measure.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let the balance be just and the weights equal, the bushel just, and the sextary equal. I am the Lord your God, that brought you out of the land of Egypt.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Keep all my precepts, and all my judgments: and do them. I am the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thus shalt thou say to the children of Israel: If any man of the children Israel, or of the strangers that dwell in Israel, give of his seed to the idol Moloch, dying let him die. The people of the land shall stone him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will set my face against him: and I will cut him off from the midst of his people, because he hath given of his seed to Moloch, and hath defiled my sanctuary, and profaned my holy name.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if the people of the land neglecting, and as it were little regarding my commandment, let alone the man that hath given of his seed to Moloch, and will not kill him:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will set my face against that man, and his kindred, and will cut off both him and all that consented with him, to commit fornication with Moloch, out of the midst of their people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The soul that shall go aside after magicians, and soothsayers, and shall commit fornication with them: I will set my face against that soul, and destroy it out of the midst of its people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Sanctify yourselves, and be ye holy: because I am the Lord your God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Keep my precepts, and do them. I am the Lord that sanctify you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He that curseth his father, or mother, dying let him die. He hath cursed his father, and mother: let his blood be upon him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If any man commit adultery with the wife of another, and defile his neighbour’s wife: let them be put to death, both the adulterer and the adulteress.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If a man lie with his stepmother, and discover the nakedness of his father, let them both be put to death: their blood be upon them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If any man lie with his daughter in law: let both die, because they have done a heinous crime. Their blood be upon them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If any one lie with a man as with a woman, both have committed an abomination: let them be put to death. Their blood be upon them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If any man after marrying the daughter, marry her mother, he hath done a heinous crime. He shall be burnt alive with them: neither shall so great an abomination remain in the midst of you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He that shall copulate with any beast or cattle, dying let him die: the beast also ye shall kill.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The woman that shall lie under any beast, shall be killed together with the same. Their blood be upon them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If any man take his sister, the daughter of his father, or the daughter of his mother, and see her nakedness, and she behold her brother’s shame: they have committed a crime. They shall be slain, in the sight of their people, because they have discovered one another’s nakedness. And they shall bear their iniquity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If any man lie with a woman in her flowers, and uncover her nakedness, and she open the fountain of her blood: both shall be destroyed out of the midst of their people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of thy aunt by thy mother, and of thy aunt by thy father. He that doth this, hath uncovered the shame of his own flesh: both shall bear their iniquity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If any man lie with the wife of his uncle by the father, or of his uncle by the mother, and uncover the shame of his near akin, both shall bear their sin. They shall die without children.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He that marrieth his brother’s wife, doth an unlawful thing: he hath uncovered his brother’s nakedness. They shall be without children.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Keep my laws and my judgments, and do them: lest the land into which you are to enter to dwell therein, vomit you also out.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Walk not after the laws of the nations, which I will cast out before you. For they have done all these things: and therefore I abhorred them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But to you I say: Possess their land which I will give you for an inheritance, a land flowing with milk and honey. I am the Lord your God, who have separated you from other people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore do you also separate the clean beast from the unclean, and the clean fowl from the unclean. Defile not your souls with beasts, or birds, or any things that move on the earth, and which I have shewn you to be unclean:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You shall be holy unto me, because I the Lord am holy: and I have separated you from other people, that you should be mine.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A man, or woman, in whom there is a pythonical or divining spirit, dying let them die. They shall stone them. Their blood be upon them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord said also to Moses: Speak to the priests the sons of Aaron, and thou shalt say to them: Let not a priest incur an uncleanness at the death of his citizens.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But only for his kin, such as are near in blood: that is to say, for his father and for his mother, and for his son, and for his daughter, for his brother also:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And for a maiden sister, who hath had no husband.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But not even for the prince of his people shall he do any thing that may make him unclean.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Neither shall they shave their head, nor their beard, nor make incisions in their flesh.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They shall be holy to their God, and shall not profane his name. For they offer the burnt offering of the Lord, and the bread of their God: and therefore they shall be holy.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They shall not take to wife a harlot or a vile prostitute, nor one that has been put away from her husband: because they are consecrated to their God,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And offer the loaves of proposition. Let them therefore be holy because I also am holy: the Lord, who sanctify them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If the daughter of a priest be taken in whoredom and dishonour the name of her father, she shall be burnt with fire.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The high priest, that is to say, the priest who is the greatest among his brethren, upon whose head the oil of unction hath been poured; and whose hands have been consecrated for the priesthood; and who hath been vested with the holy vestments. He shall not uncover his head: he shall not rend his garments.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Nor shall he go in at all to any dead person: not even for his father, or his mother, shall he be defiled.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Neither shall he go out of the holy places, lest he defile the sanctuary of the Lord: because the oil of the holy unction of his God is upon him. I am the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall take a virgin unto his wife.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But a widow or one that is divorced, or defiled, or a harlot, he shall not take: but a maid of his own people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall not mingle the stock of his kindred with the common people of this nation: for I am the Lord who sanctify him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Say to Aaron: Whosoever of thy seed throughout their families, hath a blemish, he shall not offer bread to his God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Neither shall he approach to minister to him: If he be blind; if he be lame; if he have a little, or a great, or a crooked nose;

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If his foot, or if his hand be broken;

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If he be crookbacked; or blear eyed; or have a pearl in his eye, or a continual scab, or a dry scurf in his body, or a rupture.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whosoever of the seed of Aaron the priest hath a blemish: he shall not approach to offer sacrifices to the Lord, nor bread to his God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall eat nevertheless of the loaves that are offered in the sanctuary.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Yet so that he enter not within the veil, nor approach to the altar: because he hath a blemish, and he must not defile my sanctuary. I am the Lord who sanctify them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moses, therefore spoke to Aaron, and to his sons and to all Israel, all the things that had been commanded him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Speak to Aaron and to his sons, that they beware of those things that are consecrated of the children of Israel: and defile not the name of the things sanctified to me, which they offer. I am the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Say to them and to their posterity: Every man of your race, that approacheth to those things that are consecrated, and which the children of Israel have offered to the Lord, in whom there is uncleanness, shall perish before the Lord. I am the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The man of the seed of Aaron, that is a leper, or that suffereth a running of the seed, shall not eat of those things that are sanctified to me, until he be healed. He that toucheth any thing unclean by occasion of the dead: and he whose seed goeth from him as in generation:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he that toucheth a creeping thing, or any unclean thing, the touching of which is defiling:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Shall be unclean until the evening, and shall not eat those things that are sanctified. But when he hath washed his flesh with water,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the sun is down, then being purified, he shall eat of the sanctified things, because it is his meat.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That which dieth of itself, and that which was taken by a beast, they shall not eat, nor be defiled therewith. I am the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let them keep my precepts, that they may not fall into sin, and die in the sanctuary, when they shall have defiled it. I am the Lord who sanctify them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: No stranger shall eat of the sanctified things: a sojourner of the priests, or a hired servant, shall not eat of them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he whom the priest hath bought, and he that is his servant, born in his house, these shall eat of them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If the daughter of a priest be married to any of the people, she shall not eat of those things that are sanctified nor of the firstfruits.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if she be a widow, or divorced, and having no children return to her father’s house, she shall eat of her father’s meats, as she was wont to do when she was a maid. No stranger hath leave to eat of them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He that eateth of the sanctified things through ignorance, shall add the fifth part with that which he ate, and shall give it to the priest into the sanctuary.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall not profane the sanctified things of the children of Israel, which they offer to the Lord:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Lest perhaps they bear the iniquity of their trespass, when they shall have eaten the sanctified things. I am the Lord who sanctify them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Speak to Aaron, and to his sons, and to all the children of Israel, and thou shalt say to them: The man of the house of Israel, and of the strangers who dwell with you, that offereth his oblation, either paying his vows, or offering of his own accord, whatsoever it be which he presenteth for a holocaust of the Lord,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To be offered by you: it shall be a male without blemish of the beeves, or of the sheep, or of the goats.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If it have a blemish you shall not offer it: neither shall it be acceptable.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The man that offereth a victim of peace offerings to the Lord, either paying his vows, or offering of his own accord, whether of beeves or of sheep, shall offer it without blemish, that it may be acceptable. There shall be no blemish in it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If it be blind, or broken, or have a scar or blisters, or a scab, or a dry scurf: you shall not offer them to the Lord, nor burn any thing of them upon the Lord’s altar.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: An ox or a sheep, that hath the ear and the tail cut off, thou mayst offer voluntarily: but a vow may not be paid with them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: you shall not offer to the Lord any beast that hath the testicles bruised, or crushed, or cut and taken away: neither shall you do any such things in your land.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: you shall not offer bread to your God, from the hand of a stranger, nor any other thing that he would give: because they are all corrupted, and defiled. You shall not receive them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When a bullock, or a sheep, or a goat, is brought forth, they shall be seven days under the udder of their dam: but the eighth day, and thenceforth, they may be offered to the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whether it be a cow, or a sheep, they shall not be sacrificed the same day with their young ones.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If you immolate a victim for thanksgiving to the Lord, that he may be favourable,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You shall eat it the same day. There shall not any of it remain until the morning of the next day. I am the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Keep my commandments, and do them. I am the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Profane not my holy name, that I may be sanctified in the midst of the children of Israel. I am the Lord who sanctify you:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And who brought you out of the land of Egypt, that I might be your God. I am the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Speak to the children of Israel, and thou shalt say to them: These are the feasts of the Lord, which you shall call holy.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Six days shall ye do work: the seventh day, because it is the rest of the sabbath, shall be called holy. You shall do no work on that day: it is the sabbath of the Lord in all your habitations.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These also are the holy days of the Lord, which you must celebrate in their seasons.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The first month, the fourteenth day of the month at evening, is the phase of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the fifteenth day of the same month is the solemnity of the unleavened bread of the Lord. Seven days shall you eat unleavened bread.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The first day shall be most solemn unto you, and holy: you shall do no servile work therein.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But you shall offer sacrifice in fire to the Lord seven days. And the seventh day shall be more solemn, and more holy: and you shall do no servile work therein.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Speak to the children of Israel, and thou shalt say to them: When you shall have entered into the land which I will give you, and shall reap your corn, you shall bring sheaves of ears, the firstfruits of your harvest to the priest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who shall lift up the sheaf before the Lord, the next day after the sabbath, that it may be acceptable for you, and shall sanctify it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And on the same day that the sheaf is consecrated, a lamb without blemish of the first year shall be killed for a holocaust of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the libations shall be offered with it: two tenths of flour tempered with oil, for a burnt offering of the Lord, and a most sweet odour. Libations also of wine, the fourth part of a hin.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You shall not eat either bread, or parched corn, or frumenty or the harvest, until the day that you shall offer thereof to your God. It is a precept for ever throughout your generations, and all your dwellings.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You shall count therefore from the morrow after the sabbath, wherein you offered the sheaf of firstfruits, seven full weeks.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Even unto the morrow after the seventh week be expired, that is to say, fifty days: and so you shall offer a new sacrifice to the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Out of all your dwellings, two loaves of the firstfruits, of two tenths of flour leavened, which you shall bake for the firstfruits of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you shall offer with the loaves seven lambs without blemish of the first year, and one calf from the herd, and two rams, and they shall be for a holocaust with their libations for a most sweet odour to the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You shall offer also a buck goat for sin, and two lambs of the first year for sacrifices of peace offerings.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the priest hath lifted them up with the loaves of the firstfruits before the Lord, they shall fall to his use.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you shall call this day most solemn, and most holy. You shall do no servile work therein. It shall be an everlasting ordinance in all your dwellings and generations.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when you reap the corn of your land, you shall not cut it to the very ground: neither shall you gather the ears that remain. But you shall leave them for the poor and for the strangers. I am the Lord your God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Say to the children of Israel: The seventh month, on the first day of the month, you shall keep a sabbath, a memorial, with the sound of trumpets, and it shall be called holy.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You shall do no servile work therein, and you shall offer a holocaust to the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Upon the tenth day of this seventh month shall be the day of atonement. It shall be most solemn, and shall be called holy: and you shall afflict your souls on that day, and shall offer a holocaust to the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You shall do no servile work in the time of this day: because it is a day of propitiation, that the Lord your God may be merciful unto you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Every soul that is not afflicted on this day, shall perish from among his people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And every soul that shall do any work, the same will I destroy from among his people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You shall do no work therefore on that day: it shall be an everlasting ordinance unto you in all your generations, and dwellings.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: It is a sabbath of rest, and you shall afflict your souls beginning on the ninth day of the month. From evening until evening you shall celebrate your sabbaths.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Say to the children of Israel: From the fifteenth day of this same seventh month, shall be kept the feast of tabernacles, seven days to the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The first day shall be called most solemn and most holy: you shall do no servile work therein. And seven days you shall offer holocausts to the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The eighth day also shall be most solemn and most holy: and you shall offer holocausts to the Lord. For it is the day of assembly and congregation. You shall do no servile work therein.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are the feasts of the Lord which you shall call most solemn and most holy, and shall offer on them oblations to the Lord: holocausts and libations according to the rite of every day.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Besides the sabbaths of the Lord, and your gifts, and those things that you offer by vow, or which you shall give to the Lord voluntarily.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So from the fifteenth day of the seventh month, when you shall have gathered in all the fruits of your land, you shall celebrate the feast of the Lord seven days. On the first day and the eighth shall be a sabbath: that is a day of rest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you shall take to you on the first day the fruits of the fairest tree, and branches of palm trees, and boughs of thick trees, and willows of the brook: And you shall rejoice before the Lord your God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you shall keep the solemnity thereof seven days in the year. It shall be an everlasting ordinance in your generations. In the seventh month shall you celebrate this feast.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you shall dwell in bowers seven days. Every one that is of the race of Israel, shall dwell in tabernacles:
