source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That your posterity may know, that I made the children of Israel to dwell in tabernacles, when I brought them out of the land of Egypt. I am the Lord your God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses spoke concerning the feasts of the Lord to the children of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Command the children of Israel, that they bring unto thee the finest and clearest oil of olives, to furnish the lamps continually,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Without the veil of the testimony in the tabernacle of the covenant. And Aaron shall set them from evening until morning before the Lord, by a perpetual service and rite in your generations.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They shall be set upon the most pure candlestick before the Lord continually.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt take also fine flour, and shalt bake twelve loaves thereof, two tenths shall be in every loaf.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt set them six and six, one against another, upon the most clean table before the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt put upon them the clearest frankincense, that the bread may be for a memorial of the oblation of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Every sabbath they shall be changed before the Lord: being received of the children of Israel by an everlasting covenant.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall be Aaron’s and his sons’, that they may eat them in the holy place: because it is most holy of the sacrifices of the Lord by a perpetual right.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And behold there went out the son of a woman of Israel, whom she had of an Egyptian, among the children of Israel: and fell at words in the camp with a man of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he had blasphemed the name, and had cursed it, he was brought to Moses. (Now his mother was called Salumith, the daughter of Dabri, of the tribe of Dan.)

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they put him into prison, till they might know what the Lord would command.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Saying: Bring forth the blasphemer without the camp: and let them that heard him, put their hands upon his head: and let all the people stone him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt speak to the children of Israel: The man that curseth his God, shall bear his sin:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he that blasphemeth the name of the Lord, dying let him die. All the multitude shall stone him, whether he be a native or a stranger. He that blasphemeth the name of the Lord, dying let him die.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He that striketh and killeth a man: dying let him die.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He that killeth a beast, shall make it good that is to say, shall give beast for beast.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He that giveth a blemish to any of his neighbours: as he hath done, so shall it be done to him:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Breach for breach, eye for eye, tooth for tooth, shall he restore. What blemish he gave, the like shall he be compelled to suffer.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He that striketh a beast, shall render another. He that striketh a man shall be punished.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let there be equal judgment among you, whether he be a stranger, or a native that offends: because I am the Lord your God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses spoke to the children of Israel. And they brought forth him that had blasphemed, without the camp: and they stoned him. And the children of Israel did as the Lord had commanded Moses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses in mount Sinai, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Speak to the children of Israel, and thou shalt say to them: When you shall have entered into the land which I will give you, observe the rest of the sabbath of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Six years thou shalt sow thy field and six years thou shalt prune thy vineyard, and shalt gather the fruits thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But in the seventh year there shall be a sabbath to the land, of the resting of the Lord. Thou shalt not sow thy field, nor prune thy vineyard.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: What the ground shall bring forth of itself, thou shalt not reap: neither shalt thou gather the grapes or the firstfruits as a vintage. For it is a year of rest to the land.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But they shall be unto you for meat, to thee and to thy manservant, to thy maidservant and thy hireling, and to the strangers that sojourn with thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All things that grow shall be meat to thy beasts and to thy cattle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt also number to thee seven weeks of years: that is to say, seven times seven, which together make forty-nine years.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt sound the trumpet in the seventh month, the tenth day of the month, in the time of the expiation in all your land.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt sanctify the fiftieth year, and shalt proclaim remission to all the inhabitants of thy land: for it is the year of jubilee. Every man shall return to his possession, and every one shall go back to his former family:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because it is the jubilee and the fiftieth year. You shall not sow, nor reap the things that grow in the field of their own accord, neither shall you gather the firstfruits of the vines,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because of the sanctification of the jubilee. But as they grow you shall presently eat them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the year of the jubilee all shall return to their possessions.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When thou shalt sell any thing to thy neighbour, or shalt buy of him: grieve not thy brother. But thou shalt buy of him according to the number of years from the jubilee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall sell to thee according to the computation of the fruits.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The more years remain after the jubilee, the more shall the price increase: and the less time is counted, so much the less shall the purchase cost. For he shall sell to thee the time of the fruits.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Do not afflict your countrymen: but let every one fear his God. Because I am the Lord your God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Do my precepts, and keep my judgments, and fulfil them: that you may dwell in the land without any fear.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the ground may yield you its fruits, of which you may eat your fill, fearing no man’s invasion.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if you say: What shall we eat the seventh year, if we sow not, nor gather our fruits?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will give you my blessing the sixth year: and it shall yield the fruits of three years.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the eighth year you shall sow, and shall eat of the old fruits, until the ninth year: till new grow up, you shall eat the old store.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The land also shall not be sold for ever: because it is mine, and you are strangers and sojourners with me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For which cause all the country of your possession shall be under the condition of redemption.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If thy brother being impoverished sell his little possession, and his kinsman will: he may redeem what he had sold.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if he have no kinsman, and he himself can find the price to redeem it:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The value of the fruits shall be counted from that time when he sold it. And the overplus he shall restore to the buyer, and so shall receive his possession again.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if his hands find not the means to repay the price, the buyer shall have what he bought, until the year of the jubilee. For in that year all that is sold shall return to the owner, and to the ancient possessor.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He that selleth a house within the walls of a city, shall have the liberty to redeem it, until one year be expired.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If he redeem it not, and the whole year be fully out, the buyer shall possess it, and his posterity for ever, and it cannot be redeemed, not even in the jubilee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if the house be in a village, that hath no walls, it shall be sold according to the same law as the fields. If it be not redeemed before, in the jubilee it shall return to the owner.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The houses of Levites, which are in cities, may always be redeemed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If they be not redeemed, in the jubilee they shall all return to the owners: because the houses of the cities of the Levites are for their possessions among the children of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But let not their suburbs be sold, because it is a perpetual possession.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If thy brother be impoverished, and weak of hand, and thou receive him as a stranger and sojourner, and he live with thee:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Take not usury of him nor more than thou gavest. Fear thy God, that thy brother may live with thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not give him thy money upon usury: nor exact of him any increase of fruits.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I am the Lord your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, that I might give you the land of Chanaan, and might be your God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If thy brother constrained by poverty, sell himself to thee: thou shalt not oppress him with the service of bondservants.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he shall be as a hireling, and a sojourner: he shall work with thee until the year of the jubilee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And afterwards he shall go out with his children: and shall return to his kindred and to the possession of his fathers.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For they are my servants, and I brought them out of the land of Egypt: let them not be sold as bondmen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Afflict him not by might: but fear thy God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let your bondmen, and your bondwomen, be of the nations that are round about you:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And of the strangers that sojourn among you, or that were born of them in your land. These you shall have for servants:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And by right of inheritance shall leave them to your posterity, and shall possess them for ever. But oppress not your brethren the children of Israel by might.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If the hand of a stranger or a sojourner grow strong among you, and thy brother being impoverished sell himself to him, or to any of his race:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: After the sale he may be redeemed. He that will of his brethren shall redeem him:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Either his uncle, or his uncle’s son, or his kinsman, by blood, or by affinity. But if he himself be able also, he shall redeem himself:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Counting only the years from the time of his selling unto the year of the jubilee: and counting the money that he was sold for, according to the number of the years and the reckoning of a hired servant.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If there be many years that remain until the jubilee, according to them shall he also repay the price.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If few, he shall make the reckoning with him according to the number of the years: and shall repay to the buyer of what remaineth of the years.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: His wages being allowed for which he served before: he shall not afflict him violently in thy sight.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if by these means he cannot be redeemed, in the year of the jubilee he shall go out with his children.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the children of Israel are my servants, whom I brought forth out of the land of Egypt.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I am the Lord your God. You shall not make to yourselves any idol or graven thing: neither shall you erect pillars, nor set up a remarkable stone in your land, to adore it. For I am the Lord your God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Keep my sabbaths, and reverence my sanctuary. I am the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If you walk in my precepts, and keep my commandments, and do them, I will give you rain in due seasons.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the ground shall bring forth its increase: and the trees shall be filled with fruit.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The threshing of your harvest shall reach unto the vintage, and the vintage shall reach unto the sowing time: and you shall eat your bread to the full, and dwell in your land without fear.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will give peace in your coasts: you shall sleep, and there shall be none to make you afraid. I will take away evil beasts: and the sword shall not pass through your quarters.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You shall pursue your enemies: and they shall fall before you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Five of yours shall pursue a hundred others: and a hundred of you ten thousand. Your enemies shall fall before you by the sword.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will look on you, and make you increase: you shall be multiplied, and I will establish my covenant with you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You shall eat the oldest of the old store: and, new coming on, you shall cast away the old.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will set my tabernacle in the midst of you: and my soul shall not cast you off.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will walk among you, and will be your God: and you shall be my people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I am the Lord your God: who have brought you out of the land of the Egyptians, that you should not serve them: and who have broken the chains of your necks, that you might go upright.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if you will not hear me, nor do all my commandments:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If you despise my laws, and contemn my judgments so as not to do those things which are appointed by me, and to make void my covenant:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I also will do these things to you. I will quickly visit you with poverty, and burning heat, which shall waste your eyes, and consume your lives. You shall sow your seed in vain, which shall be devoured by your enemies.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will set my face against you, and you shall fall down before your enemies: and shall be made subject to them that hate you. You shall flee when no man pursueth you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if you will not yet for all this obey me: I will chastise you seven times more for your sins.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will break the pride of your stubbornness: and I will make to you the heaven above as iron, and the earth as brass.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Your labour shall be spent in vain: the ground shall not bring forth her increase: nor the trees yield their fruit.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If you walk contrary to me, and will not hearken to me, I will bring seven times more plagues upon you for your sins.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will send in upon you the beasts of the field, to destroy you and your cattle, and make you few in number: and that your highways may be desolate.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if even so you will not amend, but will walk contrary to me:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I also will walk contrary to you, and will strike you seven times for your sins.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will bring in upon you the sword that shall avenge my covenant. And when you shall flee into the cities, I will send the pestilence in the midst of you. And you shall be delivered into the hands of your enemies,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: After I shall have broken the staff of your bread: so that ten women shall bake your bread in one oven, and give it out by weight: and you shall eat, and shall not be filled,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if you will not for all this hearken to me, but will walk against me,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will also go against you with opposite fury: and I will chastise you with seven plagues for your sins,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So that you shall eat the flesh of your sons and of your daughters.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will destroy your high places, and break your idols. You shall fall among the ruins of your idols, and my soul shall abhor you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Insomuch that I will bring your cities to be a wilderness: and I will make your sanctuaries desolate: and will receive no more your sweet odours.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will destroy your land: and your enemies shall be astonished at it, when they shall be the inhabitants thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will scatter you among the Gentiles: and I will draw out the sword after you. And your land shall be desert, and your cities destroyed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then shall the land enjoy her sabbaths all the days of her desolation. When you shall be

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the enemy’s land, she shall keep a sabbath, and rest in the sabbaths of her desolation: because she did not rest in your sabbaths, when you dwelt therein.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And as to them that shall remain of you I will send fear in their hearts in the countries of their enemies. The sound of a flying leaf shall terrify them: and they shall flee as it were from the sword. They shall fall, when no man pursueth them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall every one fall upon their brethren as fleeing from wars: none of you shall dare to resist your enemies.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You shall perish among the Gentiles: and an enemy’s land shall consume you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if of them also some remain, they shall pine away in their iniquities, in the land of their enemies: and they shall be afflicted for the sins of their fathers, and their own.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Until they confess their iniquities, and the iniquities of their ancestors, whereby they have transgressed against me, and walked contrary unto me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore I also will walk against them, and bring them into their enemies’ land until their uncircumcised mind be ashamed. Then shall they pray for their sins.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will remember my covenant, that I made with Jacob, and Isaac, and Abraham. I will remember also the land:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Which when she shall be left by them, shall enjoy her sabbaths, being desolate for them. But they shall pray for their sins, because they rejected my judgments, and despised my laws.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And yet for all that when they were in the land of their enemies, I did not cast them off altogether. Neither did I so despise them that they should be quite consumed: and I should make void my covenant with them. For I am the Lord their God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will remember my former covenant, when I brought them out of the land of Egypt, in the sight of the Gentiles, to be their God. I am the Lord. These are the judgments, and precepts, and laws, which the Lord gave between him and the children of Israel, in mount Sinai, by the hand of Moses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Speak to the children of Israel, and thou shalt say to them: The man that shall have made a vow, and promised his soul to God, shall give the price according to estimation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If it be a man from twenty years old unto sixty years old, he shall give fifty sicles of silver, after the weight of the sanctuary:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If a woman, thirty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But from the fifth year until the twentieth, a man shall give twenty sicles: a woman ten.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From one month until the fifth year, for a male shall be given five sicles: for a female three.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A man that is sixty years old or upward, shall give fifteen sicles: a woman ten.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If he be poor, and not able to pay the estimation, he shall stand before the priest: and as much as he shall value him at, and see him able to pay, so much shall he give.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But a beast that may be sacrificed to the Lord, if any one shall vow, shall be holy,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And cannot be changed: that is to say, neither a better for a worse, nor a worse for a better. And if he shall change it: both that which was changed, and that for which it was changed, shall be consecrated to the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: An unclean beast, which cannot be sacrificed to the Lord, if any man shall vow, shall be brought before the priest:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who judging whether it be good or bad, shall set the price.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Which, if he that offereth it will give, he shall add above the estimation the fifth part.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If a man shall vow his house, and sanctify it to the Lord, the priest shall consider it, whether it be good or bad: and it shall be sold according to the price, which he shall appoint.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if he that vowed, will redeem it, he shall give the fifth part of the estimation over and above: and shall have the house.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if he vow the field of his possession, and consecrate it to the Lord, the price shall be rated according to the measure of the seed. If the ground be sown with thirty bushels of barley, let it be sold for fifty sicles of silver.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If he vow his field immediately from the year of jubilee that is beginning: as much as it may be worth, at so much it shall be rated.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if some time after, the priest shall reckon the money according to the number of years that remain until the jubilee, and the price shall be abated.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if he that had vowed, will redeem his field, he shall add the fifth part of the money of the estimation, and shall possess it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if he will not redeem it, but it be sold to any other man, he that vowed it, may not redeem it any more.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For when the day of jubilee cometh, it shall be sanctified to the Lord, and as a possession consecrated, pertaineth to the right of the priest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If a field that was bought, and not of a man’s ancestors’ possession, be sanctified to the Lord:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The priest shall reckon the price according to the number of years, unto the jubilee. And he that had vowed, shall give that to the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But in the jubilee, it shall return to the former owner, who had sold it, and had it in the lot of his possession.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All estimation shall be made according to the sicle of the sanctuary. A sicle hath twenty obols.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The firstborn, which belong to the Lord, no man may sanctify and vow: whether it be bullock, or sheep, they are the Lord’s.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if it be an unclean beast, he that offereth it shall redeem it, according to thy estimation, and shall add the fifth part of the price. If he will not redeem it, it shall be sold to another for how much soever it was estimated by thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Any thing that is devoted to the Lord, whether it be man, or beast, or field, shall not be sold: neither may it be redeemed. Whatsoever is once consecrated shall be holy of holies to the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And any consecration that is offered by man, shall not be redeemed, but dying shall die.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All tithes of the land, whether of corn, or of the fruits of trees, are the Lord’s, and are sanctified to him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if any man will redeem his tithes, he shall add the fifth part of them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of all the tithes of oxen, and sheep, and goats, that pass under the shepherd’s rod, every tenth that cometh shall be sanctified to the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: It shall not be chosen neither good nor bad, neither shall it be changed for another. If any man change it: both that which was changed, and that for which it was changed, shall be sanctified to the Lord, and shall not be redeemed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are the precepts which the Lord commanded Moses for the children of Israel in mount Sinai.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner
